title: Web search via Anthropic native web_search tool (agent mode)
description: Add Anthropic's built-in web_search server tool to agent mode requests. AnthropicProvider declares the tool; AgentLoop recognizes server_tool_use and web_search_tool_result content blocks and emits toolStart/toolEnd events so ToolCallBubble can render them. Add a globe SF Symbol case + summary extraction for 'web_search' in ToolCallInfo. UI: toggle in the chat panel header alongside the Agent toggle, persisted via @AppStorage. Scope: Anthropic-only; OpenAI/FoundationModels agents won't have web access through this path. Acceptance: with the toggle on in agent mode, asking a research question triggers a web_search tool bubble with a globe icon, query summary, and disclosure showing the search results JSON.
close_reason: Anthropic native web_search server tool wired through end-to-end. LLMRequest gained serverTools: [ServerToolConfig]?; ContentBlock gained serverToolUse/serverToolResult cases; AnthropicProvider sends web_search_20250305 in the tools array (after local tools, after cache_control) and parses server_tool_use + web_search_tool_result content blocks; AgentLoop iterates blocks in document order so server tool events interleave with text; chat panel has a Web toggle (@AppStorage). ToolCallInfo gained the web_search case (globe icon, query summary). Tests: 4 deterministic AgentLoopWebSearchTests pass; LiveAgentEvals/liveWebSearchTriggers added (skips without API key).

title: UI tests for sidebar new-note, rename, and preview toggle
description: Add three focused XCUITest cases: (1) clicking the New Note toolbar button creates an Untitled row in the sidebar — regression test for the staleness bug; (2) Return-key rename on a selected row commits and the new name appears in the sidebar; (3) Cmd+Shift+P toggles between editor and MarkdownPreviewView. Requires adding accessibility identifiers to MarkdownPreviewView and the rename TextField in DocumentRow.
notes: Tests compile and register (GetTestList sees them). XCUITest runs fail with 'Timed out while enabling automation mode' both via MCP and direct xcodebuild — same error on the pre-existing EditorUITests/testAppLaunches(). This is a macOS Privacy & Security → Automation permission gate; first XCUITest run from this environment needs an interactive grant on PunkRecordsUITests-Runner.app. Not a test-code issue.
close_reason: Three XCUITest cases added, plus accessibility identifiers on MarkdownPreviewView (markdownPreview) and the DocumentRow rename TextField (renameField). Tests compile and register. Execution blocked by macOS Automation privacy gate in this environment — same error on pre-existing testAppLaunches; needs one-time interactive grant on PunkRecordsUITests-Runner.app.

title: Redesign chat: tool-call bubbles with SF Symbol icons and disclosure details
description: Chat tool calls currently render as plain text lines. Replace with distinct tool-call bubbles: SF Symbol icon per tool (vault_search→magnifyingglass, read_document→doc.text, create_note→square.and.pencil, list_documents→list.bullet), a one-line summary of what was done, and a disclosure triangle revealing the full input args and output. Tool bubbles should remain visually attributed to the AI agent (indented under the assistant identity, similar accent color).
close_reason: Tool calls now render as distinct bubbles with SF Symbol per tool, one-line summary parsed from JSON args, and a disclosure showing pretty-printed input + output. Visually attributed to the agent via a leading accent stripe. Agent stream loop refactored to interleave tool messages between assistant text chunks.

title: Markdown preview mode in editor
description: Add a preview mode toggle to the editor that renders the markdown via swift-markdown-ui (GFM). Toolbar button or keyboard shortcut (Cmd+Shift+P) switches between raw edit and rendered preview. Read-only preview for now; split-pane edit+preview is a follow-up.
close_reason: Added MarkdownPreviewView using swift-markdown-ui (.gitHub theme); Cmd+Shift+P toggle in editor toolbar swaps between raw markdown and rendered view. Frontmatter is stripped from the preview.

title: Remove the "Agent" toggle from the chat panel — agent mode is always on
description: The Agent toggle in LLMChatPanel's header is no longer wanted — agent mode should always be on. Remove the @State isAgentMode flag and the Toggle in the header. Also remove the isAgentMode branch in sendMessage; everything routes through sendAgentMessage. Knock-on: the 'Web' toggle currently has .disabled(!isAgentMode) — drop that gate. Make sure the existing testChatButtonTogglesChatPanel UI test still finds the chat input field after the layout change.
close_reason: Removed isAgentMode state, Agent toggle, and the agent-mode branch in sendMessage. Web toggle no longer gated by agent mode. MessageContext.wasAgentMode dropped along with the Issue report 'Mode:' line. Build green; integration tests pass (40).

title: System prompt updates for vault-vs-web search and citation discipline
description: Teach the agent when to prefer vault_search vs web_search (vault for stuff already captured; web for current events, external facts, anything not in the vault). Require citations inline when web results are used. Update PromptVariants to include this guidance and add evals that verify the agent picks the right tool for a given prompt.
close_reason: terse-v2 promoted as ContextBuilder default and PromptVariant.current. Adds vault_search-vs-web_search routing and inline citation discipline (vault: [[Note]], web: [Title](url)). terse-v1 retained as PromptVariant.terseV1 for historical comparison. Deterministic tests assert template wording + that ContextBuilder emits it; live eval added that requires the agent to pick vault_search over web_search for a vault-answerable prompt (skips without API key). 19 ContextBuilder + 16 Flywheel tests green.

title: Heal duplicate frontmatter IDs on vault open
description: User's vault has two files with the same frontmatter id (Untitled.md and AgentKontrol-KB.md both have id 5728D3A6-...). The path-first migration prevents UI corruption, but the duplicates still confuse repo.document(withID:) and the search-index backlink map. On vault open, scan for duplicate ids and rewrite one of each pair with a freshly generated UUID (logging the change). Or, gentler: surface duplicates in a 'Vault issues' diagnostic and let the user pick which to keep.
close_reason: FileSystemDocumentRepository.healDuplicateIDs() walks all docs, groups by id, keeps the lex-smallest path as winner, and rewrites the rest with fresh UUIDs by surgically replacing the id: line inside the frontmatter (body text containing the same UUID is preserved). Wired into AppState.openVault before index rebuild; emits a 'Healed N duplicate document ID(s) on open.' message + console log. 4 new infra tests cover: 2-way win, no-op clean vault, 3-way collision, body-text preservation. 61 infra tests pass; build green.

title: AppState orchestration tests
description: The pure helpers in FilenameHelpers and the repository-level rename/delete flow are covered, but AppState itself (selectedDocumentPath migration, eager upsert in createNewNote, duplicate-ID upsert path, selection-follows-rename) is not. Options: (a) add PunkRecordsApp as test dep — risk of @main/linker friction; (b) extract AppState orchestration into a testable Core service. Pick before implementing.
close_reason: Extracted documents+selection bookkeeping into VaultDocumentsState (pure value type in Core). AppState now delegates upsert / remove / applyRename / apply(VaultChange) to it via session.* and exposes documents/selectedDocumentPath/selectedDocument as thin shims. 15 new tests cover: path-first vs id-fallback upsert, selection-follows-rename, selection-clears-on-delete, eager-upsert-then-watcher-event coalescing, and the documented PUNK-kdm collapse limitation. 61 integration tests pass.

title: OpenAI native web_search via Responses API
description:
Mirror the Anthropic web_search plumbing on OpenAIProvider so the agent loop
can do web research when the user has chosen the OpenAI default. This is the
provider-native counterpart to PUNK-tb3, not the generic Brave/Tavily
fallback (that stays in PUNK-e5u).

What's needed:

- Recognise that the OpenAI built-in `web_search` tool is only available on
  the **Responses** endpoint (POST /v1/responses), not Chat Completions.
  OpenAIProvider.completeWithTools currently posts to Chat Completions — it
  needs a Responses code path when serverTools contains .webSearch, or a
  full move to Responses for tool-using calls.
- Translate ServerToolConfig.webSearch(maxUses:) into the Responses-tool
  shape: `{"type": "web_search"}` (or `web_search_preview` depending on
  account tier — verify against OpenAI's current docs at implementation
  time).
- Decode the response's output items. Web search results arrive as a
  `web_search_call` item (server-side tool use) and a `message` item with
  URL citation annotations attached to text segments. Map those onto the
  existing ContentBlock.serverToolUse / .serverToolResult cases so AgentLoop
  emits the same .toolStart / .toolEnd events the Anthropic path does — no
  view-layer change required.
- Surface the per-call pricing in the orchestrator's user-visible cost
  estimate (OpenAI charges roughly $25–30 per 1k searches on top of token
  usage at last public pricing).
- Tests:
  - Deterministic AgentLoop tests via ScriptedProvider asserting OpenAI's
    output-item shape round-trips through serverToolUse/Result, matching
    the existing AgentLoopWebSearchTests pattern.
  - Live eval (skips without OPENAI_API_KEY in keychain) that the agent
    actually triggers web_search for a "look up current X" prompt — mirror
    of LiveAgentEvals.liveWebSearchTriggers.

Depends on PUNK-tb3 (done) for the ServerToolConfig + ContentBlock cases.
close_reason: OpenAIProvider.completeWithTools now posts to /v1/responses (kept Chat Completions for complete/stream — Ollama/LM Studio compat). Implements: ServerToolConfig.webSearch → {type: web_search} input tool; ToolDefinition → flat function tool; ContentBlock conversation history → input items with sibling function_call / function_call_output. Decodes the output array: web_search_call → .serverToolUse(query); message output_text → .text + synthesizes .serverToolResult with the model's url_citation annotations (or a 'Search completed' placeholder if none); function_call → .toolUse with stopReason=.toolUse. 10 deterministic OpenAIProviderResponsesTests pass; live eval LiveAgentEvals.liveOpenAIWebSearchTriggers skips cleanly without an OpenAI key, runs end-to-end with one. Settings UI already accepts the OpenAI key.

title: "Save as note" should preserve web citations
description: When a chat reply was answered with web_search citations, the existing Save as Note flow should preserve them — wikilinks for vault references, footnotes or inline (Title)[url] for web sources. NoteCompiler currently treats the response text as opaque; needs to be citation-aware. Depends on web_search landing first so we have a citation shape to design against.
close_reason: saveResponseAsNote now pre-extracts wikilinks and web markdown-links from the source, instructs the LLM to preserve them in the prompt, and post-processes the LLM output: any citation that the model dropped gets recovered into an appended '## Sources' section (web links as [Title](url), wikilinks as [[target|display]]). 4 new tests cover: kept-when-preserved, dropped-web-recovery, dropped-wikilink-recovery, and partial-drop survival. 8 NoteCompiler tests green; build green.

title: Brave (or Tavily) WebSearchTool for providers without a native web_search
description:
Path-B fallback. Path A — provider-native web search — is already done for
Anthropic (PUNK-tb3) and tracked for OpenAI (PUNK-lc2). This issue only
applies to providers that don't have a server-managed web_search tool of
their own (currently FoundationModelsProvider, and any future provider that
ships without one).

What's needed:

- A WebSearchTool conforming to the local AgentTool protocol (not a
  ServerToolConfig). Returns top N URL + title + snippet for a query.
- Pluggable backend: Brave Search API as the default (cheap, keys are easy
  to get); Tavily as an alternative configurable per-vault. API key in
  Keychain, scoped by service identifier so the same key isn't reused
  across services.
- Companion WebFetchTool that pulls a single URL and reduces it to
  reading-friendly markdown (try r.jina.ai first as a hosted readability
  service; fall back to a WKWebView-based extraction if that's blocked).
- Wire-up rule in AgentLoop: when the chosen provider doesn't advertise
  capability X via LLMCapabilities (introduce .nativeWebSearch), the loop
  attaches these local tools so the agent has a path to the web regardless
  of provider.
- Tests:
  - Deterministic: ScriptedProvider exercising WebSearchTool/WebFetchTool
    against a mock URL session (no network in CI).
  - Live (tag .eval, skips without BRAVE_API_KEY in keychain): real query
    returns at least one result with title + URL.

Implement only when there's a concrete need — i.e. we want web research
from FoundationModelsProvider, or we want a vendor-agnostic fallback for
cost-sensitive sessions. Until then this stays open as a placeholder.

title: UI test: drag-out a sidebar row produces a file URL
description: Verify .draggable(URL) on a DocumentRow produces a file-promise drop. XCUITest drag-and-drop is unreliable, especially cross-app — may require an in-app drop target as a test surrogate (e.g. a hidden Test pane that accepts the drop and exposes the URL via accessibility). Risk: flaky even with that scaffolding.
close_reason: DocumentRow now mirrors its .draggable(fileURL) URL into accessibilityValue (only consumed by --ui-testing). Test asserts the sidebar cell's value ends in 'scratch-note.md' and lives under the PunkRecords-UITest temp vault. Verifies the contract — the URL bound to .draggable points to the right file — without depending on XCUITest synthesizing a drop. Tried in-app drop zones with .dropDestination(for: URL.self) and .onDrop(of: [.fileURL]), press+drag on staticText / cell / coordinates, .slow velocity + hold — none of them fire SwiftUI's drag handler from a List row on macOS. Manual drag-out to Finder still works in production. 10/10 UI tests pass.

title: Snapshot tests for MarkdownPreviewView and ToolCallBubble via mcp__xcode__RenderPreview
description: Visual regression suite for SwiftUI #Preview definitions (MarkdownPreviewView with sample markdown, ToolCallBubble in-flight/completed/error states). Use RenderPreview to capture PNGs and diff against baselines stored in the repo. Better fit than XCUITest for visual fidelity.
close_reason: Snapshot regression workflow landed: Tests/SnapshotBaselines/ holds 4 baselines captured via RenderPreview (MarkdownPreviewView_Sample + ToolCallBubble InFlight/Completed/Error). Scripts/snapshot-diff.swift decodes two PNGs to RGBA via CGImage and asserts the mean per-channel absolute difference (0–255 scale) is below a threshold (default 2.0) — tolerant of PNG metadata/compression noise but catches real visual change. Scripts/check-snapshots.sh runs the diff over a fresh-render directory matching the baselines by filename; agent workflow is in Tests/SnapshotBaselines/README.md. Tests/PunkRecordsIntegrationTests/SnapshotBaselineSanityTests.swift adds CI guards that baselines exist, decode as valid PNGs, and have reasonable bounds. End-to-end dry run: 4/4 baselines reproduce with mean_diff ≤ 0.02. Sanity tests pass.

title: UI test: delete-with-confirmation flow
description: Right-click a sidebar row → Move to Trash → confirmation dialog appears → confirm → row disappears. Needs a multi-doc UI test fixture so deleting Test Note doesn't leave an empty vault.
close_reason: Right-click → Move to Trash → Confirm flow covered in DeleteFlowUITests (2 cases: confirm removes row, cancel preserves it). UI-test vault gained a second 'Scratch Note' fixture so the destructive test doesn't empty the vault for parallel runs. SwiftUI confirmationDialog on macOS renders as a sheet, so the helper looks in app.sheets.buttons first, falling back to top-level buttons. 9/9 UI tests pass.

title: Rewind AI chat to a previous turn
description: Allow the user to roll the chat back to a previous turn (truncate later messages, including any in-flight tool calls and their effects). UI: per-message context menu with 'Rewind to here', confirmation dialog, then truncate the messages array. Should also clean up any side-effects from rewound assistant turns (e.g. notes created via CreateNoteTool — needs design).

title: Render markdown in chat bubbles
description: Assistant chat replies currently render as plain text — but the model tries to emit wikilinks and code blocks. Replace plain Text() in assistant bubbles with swift-markdown-ui's Markdown() so GFM (links, code fences, lists, tables) renders. Depends on swift-markdown-ui already being a dependency (from the editor preview work).
close_reason: ChatBubble now renders assistant messages with Markdown(.gitHub) at 0.9em; user messages remain plain Text. Build green; integration tests pass. Visual snapshot coverage tracked separately in PUNK-co8.
